question: Which of the following statements about the components of a distributed service on a Nokia 7750 SR is TRUE?
options: Each service must use a separate SDP.
An SAP can be used by multiple services on a router.
An SAP can only be created on an access port.
The VC IDs at both ends of a service can be different.
correct_answers: An SAP can only be created on an access port.

question: Consider a network that is providing MPLS VPN services. Which statement best describes packets sent from the CE router to the PE router?
options: The packets have an outer transport label and an inner service label.
The packets have an outer service label and an inner transport label.
The answer depends on the type of VPN service provided by the MPLS network.

question: A dot1Q SAP 1/1/1:* configured on an Epipe service on a Nokia 7750 SR has an MTU of 1518 bytes. What should the configured value of the service MTU be so that the Epipe carries full-size Ethernet frames received on the default SAP?
Which of the following most accurately describes when service labels are signalled for an epipe?
options: Service labels are signalled when the SDP has been created at both ends.
Service labels are signalled when the epipe service has been administratively enabled at one end.
Service labels are signalled when the epipe service has been administratively enabled at both ends.
Service labels are signalled when the epipe service has been administratively enabled and the SDP binding has been created in the service at both ends.
Service labels are signalled when the epipe service has been administratively enabled and a SAP and SDP binding have been created in the service at both ends.
correct_answers: Service labels are signalled when the epipe service has been administratively enabled and the SDP binding has been created in the service at both ends.

question: An epipe SAP on a PE router receives a frame carrying an IP packet from a CE router that is too large for the service. What action does the PE router take?
options: It discards the frame and sends a notification to the CE router.
It fragments the frame as long as the do-not-fragment bit is not set.
It truncates the frame to fit the service MTU.
